Class Stage

java.lang.Object
net.fokinatorr.sparkle.variable.AbstractVariableStorage<String>
net.fokinatorr.sparkle.stage.Stage
All Implemented Interfaces:
VariableStorage<String>
public class Stage extends AbstractVariableStorage<String>
A stage, containing backdrop, sprites and other renderable objects.

Note for Scratchers: The default stage size, unlike in Scratch, is 640x360 (16:9 ratio) and not 480x360 (4:3 ratio). The default framerate limit is also different, being 60 instead of 30.

    Represents the state of a stage.

  • Constructor Details

    • Stage

      public Stage()

  • Method Details

    • registerSprite

      public Stage registerSprite(String spriteId, Sprite sprite)
      Registers a new Sprite with the given ID.
      Parameters:
      spriteId - the sprite's ID
      sprite - the main sprite
      Returns:
      this Stage, for chaining registerXxx(...) calls
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if any argument is null
      IllegalStateException - if there already is a sprite with the given ID
      IllegalArgumentException - if the given sprite is already registered in a different stage

    • getMainSprite

      public Sprite getMainSprite(String spriteId)
      Returns the main sprite with the given ID.
      Parameters:
      spriteId - the sprite ID
      Returns:
      the main sprite with the given ID
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if the given ID is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if there is no sprite with the given ID

    • getSprite

      public Sprite getSprite(String spriteId, int cloneId)
      Returns the sprite with the given sprite ID and clone ID. Note that the main sprite always has the clone ID 0.
      Parameters:
      spriteId - the sprite ID
      cloneId - the clone ID
      Returns:
      the sprite identified by the given IDs
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if the given ID is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if there is no sprite with the given sprite ID or clone ID

    • unregisterSprite

      public it.unimi.dsi.fastutil.ints.Int2ObjectMap<Sprite> unregisterSprite(String spriteId)
      Unregisters the sprite with the given ID, i.e. removes it and its clones from the stage.
      Parameters:
      spriteId - the sprite ID
      Returns:
      an immutable view of the removed sprites by clone ID; 0 clone ID represents the main sprite
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if the sprite ID is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if there is no such sprite with the given ID

    • registerRenderable

      public Stage registerRenderable(String id, Object obj)
      Registers an arbitrary renderable object with the given ID. There must be a renderer available for the object's type to register successfully.

      To register sprites, use registerSprite(String, Sprite) instead; they're a special case of renderable objects.

      Parameters:
      id - the object's ID
      obj - the object
      Returns:
      this Stage, for chaining registerXxx(...) calls
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if any argument is null, or if the object is not renderable
      IllegalStateException - if there already is an object with the given ID
      IllegalArgumentException - if the given object is a sprite

    • getRenderable

      public <T> T getRenderable(String id)
      Returns the renderable object with the given ID. This method does not check for sprites.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the requested type of the object
      Parameters:
      id - the object ID
      Returns:
      the renderable object with the given ID
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if the given ID is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if there is no object with the given ID
      ClassCastException - if the object cannot be case to requested type

    • unregisterRenderable

      public <T> T unregisterRenderable(String id)
      Unregisters the renderable object with the given ID, i.e. removes it from the stage.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the requested type of the object
      Parameters:
      id - the object ID
      Returns:
      the unregistered object
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if the object ID is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if there is no such object with the given ID
      ClassCastException - if the object cannot be case to requested type

    • createClone

      public int createClone(String spriteId)
      Creates a clone of the sprite with the given ID. The clone is immediately added to this stage.

      Note for Scratchers: Unlike in Scratch, the clone's initial layer will be front instead of back.

      Parameters:
      spriteId - the sprite ID
      Returns:
      the clone ID of the new clone
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if there is no sprite with the given ID
      NullPointerException - if the given ID is null

    • deleteClone

      public void deleteClone(String spriteId, int cloneId)
      Deletes a clone with the given clone ID of the sprite with the given ID. If there is no clone with the given clone ID, this method does nothing.
      Parameters:
      spriteId - the main sprite ID
      cloneId - the clone ID
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if the sprite ID is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if there is no sprite with the given ID, or the clone ID is 0, which represents the main sprite (use unregisterSprite(String) to remove the main sprite with clones)

    • deleteAllClones

      public void deleteAllClones(String spriteId)
      Deletes all clones of the sprite with the given ID. If the sprite has no clones, this method does nothing.
      Parameters:
      spriteId - the main sprite ID
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if the sprite ID is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if there is no sprite with the given ID

    • moveToLayer

      public void moveToLayer(RenderableID<?> id, int newLayer)
      Moves the object with the given ID to the given layer.
      Parameters:
      id - the renderable's ID
      newLayer - the new layer of the desired object, will be clamped to be in range [0; renderable count)
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if ID is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if no object/sprite with the given ID was found
      See Also:
      • RenderableID

    • moveToFrontLayer

      public void moveToFrontLayer(RenderableID<?> id)
      Moves the object with the given ID to the front layer.

      Equivalent to moveToLayer(id, getLayers().size() - 1).

      Parameters:
      id - the renderable's ID
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if ID is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if no object/sprite with the given ID was found
      See Also:
      • RenderableID

    • moveToBackLayer

      public void moveToBackLayer(RenderableID<?> id)
      Moves the object with the given ID to the front layer.

      Equivalent to moveToLayer(id, 0).

      Parameters:
      id - the renderable's ID
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if ID is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if no object/sprite with the given ID was found
      See Also:
      • RenderableID

    • moveByLayers

      public void moveByLayers(RenderableID<?> id, int delta)
      Moves the object with the given ID delta count of layers. Negative delta stands for moving backward, positive delta stands for moving forward.
      Parameters:
      id - the renderable's ID
      delta - how much layers to move the object; moves forward if positive, or moves backward if negative, Math.abs(delta) layers. If 0, does nothing
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if delta != 0 && id == null
      IllegalArgumentException - if delta != 0 and no object/sprite with the given ID was found
      See Also:
      • RenderableID

    • getLayers

      public List<RenderableID<?>> getLayers()
      Returns an immutable List view which represents in which order to render the objects (in other words, the objects' layers). Whenever an object's layer is updated, it is reflected in the view returned by this method.
      Returns:
      a view over this stage's objects' layers
      See Also:
      • getSprite(String, int)
      • getRenderable(String)
      • RenderableID

    • getWidth

      public int getWidth()
      Returns the current width of this stage.
      Returns:
      the current width of this stage

    • getHeight

      public int getHeight()
      Returns the current height of this stage.
      Returns:
      the current height of this stage

    • setWidth

      public void setWidth(int newWidth)
      Sets the width for this stage to the given value.
      Parameters:
      newWidth - the new width
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the new width is non-positive

    • setHeight

      public void setHeight(int newHeight)
      Sets the new height for this stage to the given value.
      Parameters:
      newHeight - the new height
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the new height are non-positive

    • setSize

      public void setSize(int newWidth, int newHeight)
      Sets the width and height for this stage to the given values.
      Parameters:
      newWidth - the new width
      newHeight - the new height
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if either width or height are non-positive

    • getFPSLimit

      public int getFPSLimit()
      Returns the current framerate limit. It is not guaranteed that the actual framerate would be equal to this value; this is just the upper bound.
      Returns:
      the current framerate limit

    • setFPSLimit

      public int setFPSLimit(int fpsLimit)
      Sets the current framerate limit to the given value. It is not guaranteed that the actual framerate would be equal to this value; this is just the upper bound.
      Parameters:
      fpsLimit - the new framerate limit
      Returns:
      the old framerate limit

    • getTPSLimit

      public int getTPSLimit()
      Returns the current tick rate limit. It is not guaranteed that the actual tick rate would be equal to this value; this is just the upper bound.
      Returns:
      the current tick rate limit

    • setTPSLimit

      public int setTPSLimit(int tpsLimit)
      Sets the current tick rate limit to the given value. It is not guaranteed that the actual tick rate would be equal to this value; this is just the upper bound.
      Parameters:
      tpsLimit - the new tick rate limit
      Returns:
      the old tick rate limit

    • getState

      public Stage.State getState()
      Returns the current state of this stage.
      Returns:
      this stage's state, never null
      See Also:
      • Stage.State

    • start

      public CompletableFuture<Void> start()
      Fires the start event on this stage, then, after its dispatch completes, starts the stage's loop thread. Doesn't do anything if the stage is already running or stopping. Returns immediately.
      Returns:
      a CompletableFuture that completes when the StageStateChangeEvent.StartEvent was dispatched by every listener and the loop thread was started.

    • stop

      public CompletableFuture<Void> stop()
      Fires the stop event on this stage, then, after its dispatch completes, stops the stage's loop thread. Returns immediately.
      Returns:
      a CompletableFuture that completes when the StageStateChangeEvent.StopEvent was dispatched by every listener and the loop thread was stopped.

    • setBackdrop

      public void setBackdrop(int index)
      Sets the backdrop index to the given one. This action is not guaranteed to be done since event listeners can cancel the event. This method does not wait for the event to be processed by listeners.

      Note for Scratchers: Unlike in Scratch, the first backdrop index value is 0.

      Parameters:
      index - the backdrop index
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if no backdrop with the given index was found

    • setBackdrop

      public void setBackdrop(int index, boolean waitForListeners)
      Sets the backdrop index to the given one. This action is not guaranteed to be done since event listeners can cancel the event.

      Note for Scratchers: Unlike in Scratch, the first backdrop index value is 0.

      Parameters:
      index - the backdrop index
      waitForListeners - whether to wait until the event was processed by listeners
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if no backdrop with the given index was found

    • incrementBackdrop

      public void incrementBackdrop()
      Increments the backdrop index, wrapping around if necessary.

    • decrementBackdrop

      public void decrementBackdrop()
      Decrements the backdrop index, wrapping around if necessary.

    • setBackdrop

      public void setBackdrop(String name)
      Sets the backdrop name to the given one. This action is not guaranteed to be done since event listeners can cancel the event. This method does not wait for the event to be processed by listeners.
      Parameters:
      name - the backdrop name
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if no backdrop with the given name was found
      NullPointerException - if backdrop name is null

    • setBackdrop

      public void setBackdrop(String name, boolean waitForListeners)
      Sets the backdrop name to the given one. This action is not guaranteed to be done since event listeners can cancel the event.
      Parameters:
      name - the backdrop name
      waitForListeners - whether to wait until the event was processed by listeners
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if no backdrop with the given name was found
      NullPointerException - if backdrop name is null

    • getBackdropIndex

      public int getBackdropIndex()
      Returns the current backdrop index.

      Note for Scratchers: This method matches the functionality of "backdrop number" block.

      Returns:
      the current backdrop index

    • getBackdropName

      public String getBackdropName()
      Returns the current backdrop name.
      Returns:
      the current backdrop name

    • drawBackdrop

      public void drawBackdrop(RenderCanvas canvas)
      Draws the current backdrop onto the given canvas.
      Parameters:
      canvas - the canvas
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if canvas is null

    • startSound

      public String startSound(String soundName)
      Starts playing a sound with the given name.
      Parameters:
      soundName - the sound name
      Returns:
      the control ID of the sound. Can be used to stop the sound or change its effects.
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if there is no sound with the given name in assets.

    • startSound

      public String startSound(String soundName, boolean waitForDone)
      Starts playing a sound with the given name.
      Parameters:
      soundName - the sound name
      waitForDone - whether to wait until the sound finished playing. If set to true, the return value is essentially useless.
      Returns:
      the control ID of the sound. Can be used to stop the sound or change its effects.
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if there is no sound with the given name in assets.

    • createVariables

      protected Map<String, Variable<?>> createVariables()
      Creates a new Map to store variables in. This method must not have side effects and must solely create a new empty mutable map instance (e.g., using constructor).
      Specified by:
      createVariables in class AbstractVariableStorage<String>
      Returns:
      a new variable map
